title: Jaeger Medical Vyntus Series (ONE / SPIRO / BODY / CPX) - Calibration or Flow Sensor Error
description: Troubleshooting calibration failures or flow sensor errors on Jaeger Medical Vyntus pulmonary function testing systems.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Do not troubleshoot pulmonary testing equipment on an active patient. Stop testing immediately if calibration errors occur during a procedure. Move the patient to another functioning pulmonary testing system before continuing testing.

steps.title: Confirm the Exact Error Message
steps.instructions: Review the error message displayed in the Vyntus software. Common examples may include Calibration failed, Flow sensor error, Flow out of range, or No flow detected. Document the exact message, as this helps determine whether the issue is sensor-related, calibration-related, or software-related.

steps.title: Verify the Calibration Syringe
steps.instructions: Ensure the correct 3-liter calibration syringe is being used. Check for damaged plunger seals, air leaks, loose fittings, or an expired or uncertified syringe. Calibration accuracy depends on the syringe delivering an exact known volume. A leaking or worn syringe can trigger calibration failures.

steps.title: Inspect the Flow Sensor
steps.instructions: Remove and visually inspect the flow sensor. Check for moisture buildup, condensation, dust or debris, cracked housing, or contamination from patient testing. If moisture or debris is present, allow the sensor to dry completely and replace disposable filters or mouthpieces. Flow sensors are sensitive to contamination and can easily produce calibration errors if airflow is obstructed.

steps.title: Check Tubing and Connections
steps.instructions: Inspect all tubing between the patient interface and the sensor. Verify tubing is properly seated, no kinks or blockages exist, no disconnected hoses are present, and bacterial or viral filters are installed correctly. Improper airflow routing can cause the system to detect inaccurate flow measurements.

steps.title: Confirm Correct Sensor Selection in Software
steps.instructions: Ensure the Vyntus software recognizes the correct sensor type. Check that the correct device module is selected, the sensor is properly detected by the software, and no device communication errors are present. Restarting the software may reinitialize the sensor connection.

steps.title: Verify Environmental Conditions
steps.instructions: Pulmonary testing systems rely on accurate environmental readings. Confirm room temperature is stable, there is no extreme humidity, and environmental sensors are functioning. Large environmental fluctuations can affect calibration results.

steps.title: Restart the System
steps.instructions: Perform a full system restart. Close the Vyntus application, power down the system computer, power off the Vyntus device modules, restart the system, and relaunch the software. This can clear temporary communication errors between the sensor and software.

steps.title: Attempt Calibration Again
steps.instructions: Perform a full calibration procedure. Ensure smooth and steady syringe strokes, full 3L volume each stroke, and proper timing between strokes. Irregular calibration strokes may trigger calibration failure alarms.

CCR.Complaint: Pulmonary lab reported that the Vyntus system failed daily calibration and displayed a flow sensor error.
CCR.Cause: Inspection revealed condensation buildup inside the flow sensor and a partially obstructed bacterial filter affecting airflow.
CCR.Resolution: Sensor dried, filter replaced, tubing verified, and calibration successfully completed. System returned to service.

title: Jaeger Medical Vyntus Series (ONE / SPIRO / BODY / CPX) - Software Freeze / PC Connection Lost
description: Troubleshooting guide for Jaeger Medical Vyntus pulmonary function testing systems experiencing software freezes or lost communication with the host PC.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Before troubleshooting, ensure the pulmonary function test is not actively being performed. If the system froze during testing, the patient should be safely disconnected and testing moved to another device if available.

steps.title: Check for Complete Software Freeze
steps.instructions: Determine whether the Vyntus software interface is fully frozen or partially responsive. Attempt to move the mouse and select menus. Check if the PC desktop is still responsive. Observe whether error messages are displayed. This helps determine if the issue is with the software application or the entire PC.

steps.title: Verify PC Power and Performance
steps.instructions: Because Vyntus systems depend on the connected workstation, PC issues can cause the software to freeze. Check for high CPU or memory usage, Windows error messages, an unresponsive desktop, or other applications running in the background. If the PC appears unstable, rebooting the workstation may resolve the issue.

steps.title: Inspect USB and Interface Connections
steps.instructions: Vyntus systems typically communicate with sensors and modules through USB or interface hubs. Check USB cables between the Vyntus hardware and PC, loose or partially disconnected cables, damaged USB ports, and interface hub power if present. If possible, reseat the USB cables and confirm the connections are secure.

steps.title: Check Module Power Indicators
steps.instructions: Verify that the Vyntus hardware modules have power. Look for status LEDs on the flow module or body box interface, indicator lights on USB interface devices, and any unusual blinking patterns or absence of lights. Loss of power to a module can cause the PC software to freeze while waiting for a response.

steps.title: Restart the Vyntus Software Application
steps.instructions: If the PC remains responsive but the Vyntus software is frozen, close the Vyntus application, reopen the software, and verify that the system reconnects to the hardware modules. Many freezes are resolved with a simple application restart.

steps.title: Perform a Controlled System Restart
steps.instructions: If the software cannot be closed or the PC is unresponsive, restart the PC. Allow the Vyntus hardware modules to power cycle during the restart. Launch the software again and verify that the system reconnects to the hardware. Restarting both the PC and device interface can reestablish communication.

steps.title: Verify Device Detection After Restart
steps.instructions: Once the system is restarted, launch the Vyntus software, confirm that the system detects connected hardware modules, and attempt a calibration or test to verify communication. If the software fails to detect the device after restart, a communication issue may still exist.

steps.title: Check Network Connectivity (If Applicable)
steps.instructions: Some Vyntus systems communicate with hospital networks or patient databases. Verify Ethernet cable connections, network access, and software login capability. Network instability can sometimes cause application delays or freezes.

CCR.Complaint: User reported the Vyntus pulmonary function testing system froze during testing and the software stopped responding. PC displayed no communication with the flow module.
CCR.Cause: Inspection revealed the Vyntus software was frozen while the PC remained operational. USB communication between the Vyntus hardware module and workstation was briefly lost.
CCR.Resolution: USB connections were reseated and the PC was restarted. The Vyntus software relaunched successfully and the system reconnected to the hardware. Calibration was completed and the unit was returned to service.

helpfulDetails: Software error messages present or absent

title: Jaeger Medical Vyntus Series (ONE / SPIRO / BODY / CPX) - Inaccurate Spirometry or Lung Volume Readings
description: Troubleshooting inaccurate spirometry values or lung volume measurements on Jaeger Medical Vyntus pulmonary function testing systems.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm the device is not currently in use on a patient. Ensure the pulmonary test has been stopped and the patient is disconnected before performing troubleshooting. Pulmonary measurements must not be altered while connected to a patient.

steps.title: Verify daily calibration was performed
steps.instructions: Confirm that the system completed its required daily calibration or volume verification using the calibration syringe. Look for calibration pass/fail indicators and deviations beyond acceptable limits. Spirometry systems typically require accuracy within approximately ±3% of the calibration syringe value. If calibration has not been performed, perform a full calibration procedure and re-run verification after completion. Incorrect calibration factors can directly produce incorrect patient measurement values.

steps.title: Verify correct calibration syringe use
steps.instructions: Ensure the correct calibration syringe is used and that it is functioning properly. Check the 3-liter syringe integrity, confirm there are no leaks, and verify smooth piston movement. A damaged or leaking calibration syringe will introduce measurement errors during verification.

steps.title: Check ambient environmental data
steps.instructions: Confirm that the system is using correct environmental inputs. Verify room temperature, relative humidity, and barometric pressure. If the Vyntus system uses an ambient sensor module, ensure it is connected and functioning. If entered manually, confirm values are accurate. Incorrect ambient data can lead to incorrect lung volume calculations. Also verify the ambient sensor is not located near heating vents, in direct sunlight, or near airflow sources.

steps.title: Inspect the pneumotach or flow sensor
steps.instructions: Examine the airflow sensor assembly and check for condensation, debris, contamination, or physical damage. Moisture or condensation inside flow sensors can alter airflow measurements and produce inaccurate spirometry results. If contamination is present, clean the reusable components according to manufacturer guidelines and replace disposable filters or flow sensors if applicable.

steps.title: Check patient circuit components
steps.instructions: Inspect all patient interface components and verify the correct mouthpiece is installed. Ensure the MicroGard or filter is properly attached, tubing connections are fully seated, and check for cracks or leaks in the tubing. Small leaks in the breathing circuit can produce inaccurate lung volume readings and inconsistent spirometry curves. Replace disposable components if there is any uncertainty.

steps.title: Verify proper system warm-up and stability
steps.instructions: Ensure the system has stabilized before calibration or testing. Environmental pressure fluctuations, vibration, or room disturbances can affect calibration stability and measurement accuracy. Confirm the system has been powered on long enough to stabilize and that the room environment is stable during testing.

steps.title: Review recent maintenance or configuration changes
steps.instructions: Determine whether the issue began after software updates, flow sensor replacement, hardware module replacement, or relocation of the system. Incorrect configuration or incomplete initialization can produce abnormal readings. Restart the system software and re-run calibration if needed.

steps.title: Perform verification testing
steps.instructions: Run a verification test using the calibration syringe. Observe repeatability of measurements, variance between trials, and confirm whether readings fall within acceptable tolerance. If results remain inconsistent across repeated trials, suspect sensor or module failure.

CCR.Complaint: Respiratory Therapy reported spirometry results from Vyntus system appeared inconsistent and lower than expected across multiple patients.
CCR.Cause: Inspection found condensation and debris within the pneumotach flow sensor and calibration verification failing outside acceptable tolerance.
CCR.Resolution: Flow sensor cleaned and disposable filter replaced. Full system calibration performed with 3-L syringe. Verification test passed within acceptable limits and system returned to service.

helpfulDetails: Condition of flow sensor or pneumotach

title: Jaeger Medical Vyntus Series (ONE / SPIRO / BODY / CPX) - Network / EMR Communication Failure
description: Guide for troubleshooting Vyntus pulmonary function testers that cannot communicate with the hospital network or EMR.

steps.title: Verify the System Is Powered and the Software Is Running
steps.instructions: Confirm the Vyntus PC workstation and pulmonary software are fully powered on and functioning normally. Look for software error messages, frozen application windows, delayed system response. If the software is not functioning normally, restart the workstation and reopen the Vyntus software before continuing.

steps.title: Confirm the Network Connection on the PC
steps.instructions: Check that the PC connected to the Vyntus system has an active network connection. Verify Ethernet cable is securely connected, network port indicator lights are active, the PC shows a valid network connection in Windows. If the network connection is lost, reconnect the cable or move to a known working network port.

steps.title: Verify the PC Has a Valid IP Address
steps.instructions: Open the network settings or command prompt and confirm the workstation has a valid IP address assigned by the hospital network. Look for valid IP address, proper subnet, default gateway. An invalid or missing IP address may indicate a network configuration or DHCP issue.

steps.title: Confirm EMR Interface Settings in the Vyntus Software
steps.instructions: Open the configuration settings within the pulmonary testing software and verify the interface settings. Check EMR interface enabled, correct interface server address, correct port configuration, correct protocol settings (HL7 or other interface). Incorrect settings may prevent communication with the hospital integration engine.

steps.title: Verify Communication With the Interface Engine
steps.instructions: Many Vyntus systems communicate with the EMR through an integration engine such as Cerner Connectivity Engine or similar middleware. Check for interface connection status within the software, interface error logs, failed message queues. If possible, attempt a test message or patient record transmission.

steps.title: Confirm Patient Demographic Import Function
steps.instructions: If the system normally retrieves patient data from the EMR, attempt a patient lookup. Observe whether the lookup function works, any returned error messages, slow response from the system. Failure here often indicates a server communication issue rather than a device problem.

steps.title: Restart the Vyntus Software and Workstation
steps.instructions: If communication appears intermittent, restart the system. Steps: close the pulmonary testing software, restart the workstation, reopen the application and attempt communication again. This clears temporary network or software interface faults.

steps.title: Verify the Hospital Network Is Operational
steps.instructions: Check with IT or verify another networked workstation in the same room. Confirm other devices on the network function normally, no current network outage, no recent network configuration changes. Network outages frequently appear as device communication failures.

CCR.Complaint: Pulmonary lab reported that the Vyntus system could not upload test results to the EMR and patient demographics were not importing.
CCR.Cause: Network cable at the workstation was found partially disconnected, causing intermittent network communication.
CCR.Resolution: Reseated the Ethernet cable and verified network connectivity. Confirmed successful patient lookup and test result upload to the EMR.

helpfulDetails: Test message or patient lookup result

title: Jaeger Medical Vyntus Series (ONE / SPIRO / BODY / CPX) - Patient Interface Not Detected
description: Guide for troubleshooting situations where the Vyntus system fails to detect mouthpiece, mask, or flow sensor.

steps.title: Ensure Device Power and Boot Completion
steps.instructions:
Confirm the Vyntus device is powered on and has fully booted.
Why: Some sensors and interface detection systems initialize only after a complete boot.

steps.title: Inspect the Patient Interface for Proper Seating
steps.instructions:
Verify that the mouthpiece, mask, or flow sensor is fully seated in the device connector.
Check for any obstructions, residue, or moisture.
Why: A loose or improperly seated interface often prevents detection.

steps.title: Check for the Correct Interface Type
steps.instructions:
Confirm that the interface in use is compatible with the Vyntus model.
Some models require model-specific adapters for masks or mouthpieces.
Why: Incompatible accessories may not be electrically or mechanically recognized.

steps.title: Examine Flow Sensor / Interface Cable Connections
steps.instructions:
Ensure all sensor cables are fully connected at both ends.
Inspect for visible damage or kinks.
Why: Faulty or disconnected cables prevent the system from detecting the interface.

steps.title: Swap with a Known-Good Interface or Flow Sensor
steps.instructions:
Replace the current mouthpiece/mask or flow sensor with a verified working unit.
Why: Confirms whether the problem is with the accessory or the device itself.

steps.title: Restart the Device After Interface Check
steps.instructions:
Power cycle the Vyntus system with the interface properly connected.
Why: Some detection errors are resolved after a restart once the device senses the interface correctly.

steps.title: Check for Software Prompts or Error Messages
steps.instructions:
Observe any on-screen messages indicating the type of detection failure.
Follow any recommended prompts for recalibration or interface verification.

CCR.Complaint: The Vyntus system does not detect the mouthpiece or mask when attempting spirometry.
CCR.Cause: Observed the interface was not seated properly; cable connections intact but device failed to recognize accessory.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced with a known-good mouthpiece; confirmed detection; system now ready for patient testing.

helpfulDetails: Flow sensor or interface serial numbers used
